agent.help: Run official Underlord agent editing on a project.

options.name: --new
options.help: Create new project with default name

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management. Uses the official descript-api CLI configuration. `descript auth login` delegates to `descript-api config set api-key`, and `descript auth status` validates the official CLI config.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure the official Descript API CLI API key. Auto-provisions the official descript-api CLI when needed. Use `descript config set api-key` for direct config access.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth logout --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.
remove.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles remove --help` for exact arguments and options.

rename.help: Rename a profile, re-keying its secrets to the new profile name.

arguments.name: new
arguments.help: New profile name (must not already exist)

options.name: --keep-old
options.help: Build and activate the new profile but leave the old profile dir and old secrets intact

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.
select.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles select --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Validate the official Descript API CLI configuration and report authentication status across profiles.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `descript cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `descript cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

compositions.usage_instructions: Manage compositions within Descript projects. List compositions, get details, list video assets, and export videos. PROJECT_ID (UUID) is required for all operations. Use full IDs from JSON output for follow-up commands, not shortened table cells.

active.help: Show compositions currently visible in the Descript desktop app.

options.name: --project-id
options.help: Only include active pages for this project UUID

assets.help: List video assets in a project for export. Shows the raw video recordings available in the project. Use the asset ID with 'descript compositions export' to download. Example: descript compositions assets <project-id> descript compositions assets <project-id> --table
assets.usage_instructions: List raw video recording assets in a project. Use the asset ID from JSON output with `compositions export` to download raw recordings. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument copied from JSON project output. Treat --table as display-only because long UUIDs can be shortened by the terminal renderer.

delete.help: Delete a composition from a project via Descript app automation.

export.help: Export a video asset or composition as MP4. Two export modes: 1. Asset export (raw recording via API media segment URLs): descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id>. Rate-limited or interrupted downloads can be resumed by rerunning the same command. 2. Composition export (full video with slides via Descript app): descript compositions export <project-id> --composition m2c1 -o ./m2c1.mp4. Composition export requires Descript to be launched with CDP on port 9222. If the target project is not open, the CLI auto-opens it via the descript://project/<project-id> deep link and waits up to 60 seconds for the project page to appear. It automates the app's local export to produce the full rendered video. Example: descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id> descript compositions export <project-id> --composition m2c1 -o ./m2c1.mp4 descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id> --fps 60 --width 3840 --height 2160
export.usage_instructions: Export video in two modes: (1) Asset export (raw recording via API media segment URLs) — pass PROJECT_ID and ASSET_ID as positional args. Raw asset export does not use the Descript desktop export UI. If rate limiting interrupts the download, rerun the same command; the CLI resumes from matching output.mp4.part and output.mp4.part.json state. (2) Composition export (full rendered video with slides) — pass PROJECT_ID and use --composition/-C with the composition name or ID. Composition export requires Descript launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222. The CLI auto-opens the target project via the descript://project/<project-id> deep link when it is not already open and polls CDP up to 60 seconds for the project page; it fails loudly if the page never appears. Composition export also requires macOS assistive (Accessibility) access for the responsible host app of the calling process (e.g. when run from Claude desktop, the embedded Claude Code runtime bundle at ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/<version>/claude.app — shown as lowercase 'claude' in the Accessibility list, distinct from capital-C 'Claude'; or Terminal/iTerm for shells); the CLI probes this before triggering the export and fails fast with grant guidance (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility) when access is missing. The calling app additionally needs Full Disk Access (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Full Disk Access) — a separate grant from Accessibility. The CLI activates Descript (brings it frontmost) before clicking Export because the native save sheet only appears when Descript is the frontmost app; do not run other foreground UI automation during an export. Preflight: quit Descript, wait for pgrep -x Descript to return no process, run open -na "Descript" --args --remote-debugging-port=9222, then verify http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version returns HTTP 200 before export. Full composition exports wait up to 5 minutes for Descript's native save sheet, then up to 30 minutes after accepting the save dialog before timing out. Default resolution is 1920x1080 at 30fps.

arguments.name: asset_id
arguments.help: Video asset ID for raw export (use 'descript compositions assets' to find)

options.name: --composition
options.help: Composition name or ID to export via Descript app

options.name: --audio
options.help: Export audio only as WAV (alias for --format wav)

get.help: Get a specific composition from a project. Example: descript compositions get <project-id> <composition-id>
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific composition. Requires both PROJECT_ID and COMPOSITION_ID as positional arguments (UUIDs).

list.help: List compositions in a project. Example: descript compositions list <project-id> descript compositions list <project-id> --table descript compositions list <project-id> --filter "name:m1c1"
list.usage_instructions: List all compositions in a project. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument (UUID). Use --filter/-f to search by name (e.g., 'name:contains:m1c1'). Default limit is 100. Use default JSON output with --properties id,name when a composition ID will be copied into a follow-up command; do not copy IDs from table output that contains an ellipsis.

config.help: Manage official Descript API CLI configuration

profile.help: Switch to an official Descript API CLI profile.

profiles.help: List official Descript API CLI profiles.

validate.help: Validate the official Descript API CLI API key.

export-transcript.help: Export a transcript through the official CLI.

help.help: Show official Descript API CLI help.

import.help: Import media, sequences, and compositions with the official CLI.

options.name: --media
options.help: Media URL(s) or local file path(s)

options.name: --folder
options.help: Folder path for a new project

options.name: --team-access
options.help: Team access level; required for --folder imports (edit, comment, or view)

options.name: --new
options.help: Create new project with default name

job-cancel.help: Cancel an official Descript API job.

list-folders.help: List folders through the official Descript API CLI.

list-projects.help: List projects through the official Descript API CLI.

monitor.usage_instructions: Monitor and debug Descript app network activity. Captures all API calls for endpoint discovery and debugging. Logs to ~/.descript/monitor.log.

start.help: Start monitoring Descript network activity. Captures all API calls made by the Descript app for endpoint discovery and debugging. Writes to ~/.descript/monitor.log. Example: descript monitor start descript monitor start --foreground
start.usage_instructions: Start capturing Descript app API calls in the background. Use --foreground/-F to run in foreground instead. Logs to ~/.descript/monitor.log.

options.name: --foreground
options.help: Run in foreground instead of background

status.help: Check if the monitor is running. Example: descript monitor status
status.usage_instructions: Check whether the network monitor is currently running.

stop.help: Stop the network monitor. Example: descript monitor stop
stop.usage_instructions: Stop the background network monitor process.

tail.help: Show recent monitor log output. Example: descript monitor tail descript monitor tail -n 100 descript monitor tail --follow
tail.usage_instructions: Show recent lines from the monitor log. Default is 50 lines. Use --lines/-n to change count. Use --follow/-f for live streaming.

projects.usage_instructions: List and inspect Descript projects. Use default JSON `projects list --properties id,name` to find full project IDs needed for composition and export operations. Do not copy UUIDs from table output that contains an ellipsis.

get.help: Get a specific project by ID. Example: descript projects get <project-id> descript projects get <project-id> --table
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a project. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument (UUID).

list.help: List your Descript projects. Example: descript projects list descript projects list --limit 10 descript projects list --table descript projects list --filter "name:contains:Cursor" descript projects list -p "name,composition_count" descript projects list -p "name,owner.email"
list.usage_instructions: List all Descript projects. Default limit is 20. Use --filter/-f to search by name (e.g., 'name:contains:Cursor'). Use --properties/-p with dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'name,owner.email'). Use default JSON output with --properties id,name when a project ID will be copied into a follow-up command; do not copy IDs from table output that contains an ellipsis.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of projects to list

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to display (supports dot notation: owner.email)

options.name: --name
options.help: Filter projects whose name contains this text

options.name: --created-by
options.help: Filter by creator user ID; use 'me' for current user

options.name: --created-after
options.help: Filter projects created after this ISO 8601 timestamp

options.name: --created-before
options.help: Filter projects created before this ISO 8601 timestamp

options.name: --updated-after
options.help: Filter projects updated after this ISO 8601 timestamp

options.name: --updated-before
options.help: Filter projects updated before this ISO 8601 timestamp

options.name: --sort
options.help: Sort field: name, created_at, updated_at, last_viewed_at

options.name: --cursor
options.help: Pagination cursor from a previous response

publish.help: Publish a project composition through the official CLI.

update.help: Check for official Descript API CLI updates.

usage_instructions: Use for managing Descript projects, compositions, and video exports. Commands follow `descript <group> <action>` pattern. Auth uses the official descript-api CLI configuration and auto-provisions the official CLI when needed. The Descript desktop app is only required for desktop-only export helpers and monitor operations. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. Use JSON output, not --table, whenever IDs will be copied into follow-up commands.
